title: Rich Polish Oats Gratin
description: A garlicky, molten, cozy Polish-style dish made with oats, mushrooms, and vegan mozzarella, egg-free and grill-topped for extra crunch.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F and set a grill pan over medium heat.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté minced garlic and chopped onion until translucent.
Add sliced mushrooms and grated carrot to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in rolled oats, salt, and pepper, allowing the oats to toast slightly for 2 minutes.
Pour oat milk and vegetable broth into the skillet, bring to a gentle simmer, and cook until the liquid is absorbed and oats are tender, about 10 minutes.
Transfer the oat mixture to a greased baking dish and top evenly with shredded vegan mozzarella.
Place the dish in the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes until the cheese is golden and molten.
While baking, lightly toast the remaining oats on the grill pan until golden brown, then sprinkle over the gratin for added crunch.
Remove from oven, let rest for 5 minutes, then garnish with chopped parsley before serving.
Serve hot for a comforting, egg-free breakfast or brunch.
